_comment: Cross-lingual enrichment work-list applied by enrich_xling.py: per card id, English ask phrasings to ADD (deduped) and an optional English reply to set when the card has none. Authored to close the EN-recall gaps xling-check measures. Grounded in the card's own Italian source — translations of the same fact, not new claims. Append ids here as the gate surfaces more gaps; re-run enrich_xling.py then build_akb2.py.

wiki.it.crittografia.reply_en: Cryptography is the branch of cryptology dealing with 'hidden writing' — the methods used to make a message unintelligible to anyone not authorized to read it, thereby ensuring, in modern terms, the confidentiality, integrity and authenticity of the information.

elec.voltage.reply_en: Electrical voltage, often simply called voltage, is a physical quantity proportional to the amount of energy needed to move an electric charge between two points in space. By Ohm's law (V=R·I): give me any two values and I can work out the third.

ask_en: what is a derivative in calculus
wiki.it.derivata.reply_en: In mathematics, the derivative is a function that represents the rate of change of a given function with respect to a certain variable — that is, the measure of how much the value of a function changes as its argument varies.

wiki.it.inflazione.reply_en: In economics, inflation (from the Latin inflatio, 'swelling') is the increase in the general level of prices of goods and services over a given period of time.
